title: Managing IS Amidst IT Chaos: A May 1993 Seminar for Land O'Lakes
author: Peter S. Kastner (Aberdeen Group)
abstract: Aberdeen Group VP Peter Kastner's May 1993 IS strategy seminar for agricultural coop Land O'Lakes covers industry megatrends (50% of capex on IT, online data as standard), six mainframe alternative strategies (consolidate, cheap MIPS, outsource, freeze budgets, downsize with replicated systems, non-MIS departmental installations), the no-free-downsizing-lunch thesis, and principles for long-term success. Cites Andy Grove (Wall Street Journal 18 January 1993) and includes a 1992 Large Broker PC Purchases reference data slide. Direct evidence of Kastner's mainframe-alternative thesis being delivered to non-IT enterprise clients in 1993, predating the MFASC'92 (HP Brazil) conference message and contemporaneous with Aberdeen's broader downsizing research.
importance_rationale: Concrete client-engagement artifact showing Kastner's downsizing /IS-strategy framework being delivered to a non-IT enterprise (agricultural cooperative) in May 1993; pairs with Aberdeen DOPS and customer-driven decks from same era.
relevance_rationale: Documents Kastner-authored Aberdeen client work in the formative 1992-1993 mainframe-alternative period; concrete title/affiliation snapshot (Vice President, Aberdeen Group, Inc., 92 State Street, Boston).
prescience_rationale: Six-strategy framework anticipates outsourcing growth (1990s-2000s), departmental shadow-IT (2000s), and the eventual consolidation/data-center playbook used in cloud migrations (2010s+); also flags 'no free lunch' costs that became canonical in cloud migration economics.
